title: Packera spellenbergii
intro: Packera spellenbergii, also known as Spellenberg's ragwort or mountain ragwort, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving digestive issues and reducing oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Packera spellenbergii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Packera spellenbergii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
taxonomy: Packera spellenbergii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is commonly known as the Spellenberg's mountain mint or Spellenberg's ragwort. Packera spellenbergii belongs to the genus Packera and is part of the subfamily Carduoideae. The species was named after the American botanist David J. Spellenberg.
